---
title: "Wine Chemistry and Correlates of Quality"
subtitle: "Stat 184 Final Project"
author: "Jason Kaufmann, Akshith Kachakayala, Joshua Tanyi"
date: "May 5, 2026"
date-modified: now
format:
  pdf:
    embed-resources: true
    number-sections: true
    toc: true
    toc-depth: 4
    toc-location: right
    fig-align: center
    cap-location: top
    link-external-newwindow: true
    code-overflow: wrap
execute: 
  warning: false
---

## Introduction

### Objective

Quarto enables you to weave together content and executable code into a finished document. To learn more about Quarto see <https://quarto.org>.

### The Dataset

Text

## Wine Chemistry by Color

### Similar Characteristics

When you click the **Render** button a document will be generated that includes both content and the output of embedded code. You can embed code like this:

### Stark Contrasts

Text

## Correlates of Quality

### Strongest Predictors of Quality

Text

### Weakest Predictors of Quality

Text

### Varying Predictors of Quality by Color

Text

## Conclusion

### Takeaways

Text

### Next Steps

Text

## Code Appendix

## Conclusion

```{r}
1 + 1
```

You can add options to executable code like this

```{r}
#| echo: false
2 * 2
```

The `echo: false` option disables the printing of code (only output is displayed).
